pattern: Parsing the expression into a sum of monomials over a polynomial ring lets addition, subtraction, and multiplication combine like terms, which are then emitted by descending degree and lexicographic order.
hints: Represent every partial result as a map from a sorted tuple of variable factors to an integer coefficient, substituting known variables as constants.
Evaluate with the usual precedence: recurse on parentheses, multiply within a term, then add and subtract terms, combining coefficients of identical variable tuples.

Multiplying two polynomials multiplies each pair of monomials (concatenate and re-sort their factor lists, multiply coefficients); finally drop zero coefficients and sort the surviving terms by descending degree then lexicographically by their factor tuple.

edge_inputs.description: substitute one variable, a constant survives
product of two binomials with no substitutions
pure constant arithmetic collapses to one number
terms that cancel to zero are dropped
a squared term from multiplying a variable by itself
expression evaluating to zero yields an empty list

stress.description: randomly generated valid expressions over a few variables with some substitutions
stress.note: generated expressions stay within the length cap and use only legal tokens
